latest_coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN. The session egress proxy returned HTTP 403 (organization policy denial) on the CONNECT tunnel for EVERY WebFetch host this run - verified against all nine Tier-1 portals, all Tier-2/Tier-3 buyer domains, and even a neutral control URL (en.wikipedia.org), and corroborated by the proxy's recentRelayFailures log. WebFetch is therefore unusable; only WebSearch was available, which returns indexed result snippets but not live pages. Consequently NO tender detail page or specification document could be opened, and sources_checked reflects survey-via-search, not pages actually fetched. Tier 1 (all nine jurisdictions), Tier 2 (GBE/public sector/councils/universities/health) and the mandatory Tier 3 (private sector + new-source discovery) were all attempted via WebSearch. Twelve net-new opportunities were captured at HEADLINE level only from public AusTender / Buying-for-Victoria search snippets (real ATM IDs and deep links): ten Commonwealth AusTender ATMs (four Defence DSRG refurbishment RFTs at WA/Mt Waverley/Armidale/Dandenong South; DFAT airport facilitation; DFAT Vanuatu infrastructure adviser; Services Australia Doomadgee agent-services EOI; IHACPA events management; Geoscience Australia geoscience BI; DFAT DEV+ Panel; AACAP airfield design) and one Victorian DoT&P traffic-signal RFT. All are flagged pending-detail and require re-verification once egress is restored. Tier 3 produced strong NEW-SOURCE discovery (18 new sources incl. Workday Strategic Sourcing at APA, SAP Ariba sibling buyers Western Power/Arrow Energy/Energy Queensland, Coupa at Westpac, and TenderLink at Sydney Airport).

latest_run_metadata.coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN. The session egress proxy returned HTTP 403 (organization policy denial) on the CONNECT tunnel for EVERY WebFetch host this run - verified against all nine Tier-1 portals, all Tier-2/Tier-3 buyer domains, and even a neutral control URL (en.wikipedia.org), and corroborated by the proxy's recentRelayFailures log. WebFetch is therefore unusable; only WebSearch was available, which returns indexed result snippets but not live pages. Consequently NO tender detail page or specification document could be opened, and sources_checked reflects survey-via-search, not pages actually fetched. Tier 1 (all nine jurisdictions), Tier 2 (GBE/public sector/councils/universities/health) and the mandatory Tier 3 (private sector + new-source discovery) were all attempted via WebSearch. Twelve net-new opportunities were captured at HEADLINE level only from public AusTender / Buying-for-Victoria search snippets (real ATM IDs and deep links): ten Commonwealth AusTender ATMs (four Defence DSRG refurbishment RFTs at WA/Mt Waverley/Armidale/Dandenong South; DFAT airport facilitation; DFAT Vanuatu infrastructure adviser; Services Australia Doomadgee agent-services EOI; IHACPA events management; Geoscience Australia geoscience BI; DFAT DEV+ Panel; AACAP airfield design) and one Victorian DoT&P traffic-signal RFT. All are flagged pending-detail and require re-verification once egress is restored. Tier 3 produced strong NEW-SOURCE discovery (18 new sources incl. Workday Strategic Sourcing at APA, SAP Ariba sibling buyers Western Power/Arrow Energy/Energy Queensland, Coupa at Westpac, and TenderLink at Sydney Airport).
gaps: EGRESS POLICY BLOCK (dominant gap): the session egress gateway returned HTTP 403 on the CONNECT tunnel for ALL WebFetch destinations - confirmed for tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw.gov.au, tenders.nsw/vic/sa/tas/act.gov.au, tenders.wa.gov.au, qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au, tendersonline.nt.gov.au, and every Tier-2/Tier-3 buyer domain, plus a neutral control (en.wikipedia.org). Per the proxy README these are organization egress-policy denials and were not retried/routed around. No detail page or specification document could be read this run. ACTION: the session egress allowlist must permit AU government/buyer domains (e.g. *.gov.au, vendorpanel.com.au, *.tenderlink.com, SAP Ariba/Coupa hosts) before the routine can capture full deliverables/requirements again.

All 12 captured opportunities are HEADLINE-ONLY (from public search snippets): full scope, deliverables, evaluation criteria, contacts, documents and values were not captured. They need a detail pass once fetch access is restored.
AusTender GA2026/818, DFAT-1050 (DEV+ Panel) and AACAP 2026-P04: closing dates not present in snippets; DFAT-1050 may already be in evaluation - status to verify.

QLD QTenders leads seen only via an aggregator (qld2032.com), not QTenders itself, so NOT recorded as confirmed tenders: 'Everton Electorate Office Fitout and Relocation' (Qld Parliamentary Service, ~close 13-Jul-2026) and 'Lease and operate new long day care service - Chinchilla' (Qld Dept of Education, ~close 24-Jul-2026). Verify on qtenders.hpw.qld.gov.au next run.

Tenders Tasmania 'EDSS support/hosting/maintenance' (~close 31-Jul-2026) seen in snippet but likely the same as already-known TendersTAS:edss-2026 - excluded as a probable duplicate pending verification.
NT QTOL: a Dept of Chief Minister & Cabinet tender (close 27-Jul-2026 02:00 PM ACST) appears to exist but title/ID/category were not extractable from the snippet - deferred.

Tenders WA, Tenders SA/Buying4SA and ACT Tenders: no individual current opportunity was extractable via search this run (WA showed only a scheduled-outage notice 1-Jul-2026; SA search was polluted by South-African 'sa-tenders' noise) - deferred.
buy.nsw / NSW eTendering: no net-new NSW opportunity surfaced this run; only previously-known items reappeared in snippets.

SAP Ariba Discovery (service.ariba.com/Discovery.aw): per SAP docs, viewing/responding requires a (free) seller account - it is NOT an open public board. No publicly-visible AU postings capturable without login; recorded as portal-registration only.
Bank procurement (CBA, NAB, ANZ on SAP Ariba; Westpac on Coupa) is invitation/registration-gated with no public RFx board - portals recorded, individual opportunities not captured (no fabrication).

Energy Queensland (Ergon/Energex) public 'Current tenders' pages and the two airport portals (Sydney Airport TenderLink, WSI Airport) may carry publicly-advertised RFx but could not be read due to 403 - highest-value targets to re-attempt once egress is restored.
Tier 2 (GBE/councils/universities/health): no opportunity could be verified because every buyer domain and VendorPanel were 403-blocked; coverage deferred to a future run with fetch access.
recommended_seed_additions: Origin Energy - Origin Vendor Portal (https://www.originenergy.com.au/about/procurement/) - free self-registration, regional SME program.
Western Power - SAP Ariba supplier portal (WA electricity network).
Energy Queensland (Ergon Energy / Energex) - dual VendorPanel(advertise)+SAP Ariba(respond); public Current tenders pages - check weekly.
APA Group - Workday Strategic Sourcing (new platform family; seed for detecting Workday-Sourcing sibling buyers).
Sydney Airport Corporation - TenderLink e-tendering portal (buyer-hosted); seed for other TenderLink sibling buyers.
Arrow Energy and Western Power as SAP Ariba sibling buyers; Westpac as a Coupa sibling buyer (alongside known Qantas).
Lendlease Site Access and John Holland Contractors as subcontractor onboarding portals (packages flow via EstimateOne).
INFRASTRUCTURE: add AU government/buyer domains to the session egress allowlist so WebFetch can open detail pages again - without this the routine can only capture headline-level data.

tenders.description: Inner West Council invites expressions of interest from suitably qualified and experienced organisations for the operation and management of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Operation and ongoing management of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre as a community facility — the successful organisation will run the centre and deliver community programs/services on the Council's behalf.
key_deliverables: Operation of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre
Management of the community facility and its programs/services
requirements: Suitably qualified and experienced organisation (community/neighbourhood centre operation)
tenders.eligibility: Open EOI to suitably qualified and experienced organisations (publicly advertised via VendorPanel).
tenders.submission_method: Lodge via VendorPanel (www.vendorpanel.com.au/innerwest/tenders); enquiries via the VendorPanel online forum
tenders.delivery_location: Summer Hill, Inner West, Sydney, NSW
tenders.detail_source: Inner West Council current-tenders / VendorPanel search-result snippet (title, EOI, closing Mon 29-Jun-2026 10:00 AEST, contact Frank Tambosis); council page returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch.
tenders.reasoning: Found via VendorPanel public-tenders / Inner West Council current-tenders search; buyer-originated (council) on its chosen VendorPanel platform.
tenders.notes: Closing-soon (29-Jun-2026 10:00 AEST). Contact name from the listing snippet; email/phone routed via the VendorPanel forum. Tender reference, value and full scope unverified (page not openable, 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE sweep; re-checked this run (Quakers Hill WRRF Package C + Strategic Customer Engagement market sounding).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical seed; queried this run for June/July 2026 ATMs (OPC-2026-02 printing RFT captured).
discovered_sources.notes: Detail pages returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run; data via search snippets only.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical seed; queried this run (open + 'new this week' views).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical seed; central WA public-sector RFT portal (Main Roads WA, Water Corporation, regional commissions).

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier 3 channel; re-checked this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Public posting board for corporate RFx; full opportunity detail requires a free SAP Business Network supplier account. No publicly visible AU opportunity captured without login this run.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Known major buyer; re-checked this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Opportunities via SAP Ariba + GCMS (interestedsupplier.bhp.com); registration-gated, no public RFx captured.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 sweep of transport/logistics buyers.
discovered_sources.notes: Engagement via email + Coupa Supplier Portal; no public RFP list. Opportunities are invitation/registration based.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 sweep of retail buyers.
discovered_sources.notes: Supplier onboarding portal; live opportunities handled via relationship managers, not publicly listed.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 sweep of retail buyers.
discovered_sources.notes: All Coles tenders are conducted via the Ariba Network (free registration); RangeMe is the product-supplier discovery channel. Individual tenders are login-gated.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical seed; open-opportunities search yielded several July-2026 tenders this run (incl. new Randwick High School fibre upgrade).
discovered_sources.notes: Listing rows readable via search snippets; both buy.nsw and the legacy tenders.nsw.gov.au RSS/detail pages returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 WA GBE sweep; Water Corporation runs tendering via SAP Ariba and advertises on Tenders WA. 2026 Engineering Design Services Panel early tender advice issued.
discovered_sources.notes: Major capital works and panel opportunities; Ariba registration required to view/participate.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 sweep; Main Roads WA posts opportunities and Early Tender Advice via Tenders WA and a buyer-hosted e-tendering portal.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated state road agency. Portal is buyer-hosted (not a paid aggregator); registration required to access documents.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 1 SA sweep; SA Government opportunities portal complementing tenders.sa.gov.au.
discovered_sources.notes: Modern SA opportunities portal; pair with tenders.sa.gov.au for full SA coverage.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 1 QLD sweep; destination for the QTenders-to-VendorPanel migration (phased through 2025–2026).
discovered_sources.notes: Suppliers must register with VendorPanel to access documents and respond; check alongside the legacy QTenders directory (qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE sweep; re-checked this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Government Business Enterprise buyer; individual opportunities may require registration/contact.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE sweep; re-checked this run (a Design and Construction RFP referenced on NBN's media centre).
discovered_sources.notes: Largely invitation/contact-based (supplierpolicies@nbnco.com.au); recorded as a source, opportunities not publicly listed.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE sweep; Snowy Hydro runs renewable-energy and Snowy 2.0-related procurement; current tenders surfaced via the SERREE opportunities listing (discovery signal).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated channel; confirm the live tenders page directly. SERREE used only as a discovery signal, not as the source of truth.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 mining sweep; Fortescue requires supplier pre-qualification and sources local suppliers via ICN/ePilbara for projects (Iron Bridge, Decarbonisation, Gibson Island).
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated; individual sourcing events require an authenticated account. No public RFx captured.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 energy sweep; Woodside uses a Supplier Engagement & SAP Ariba portal for registration, qualification and RFx.
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated (Ariba Network/Catalogues/We-buy). No public RFx captured.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 energy sweep; Santos uses a Coupa portal for supplier onboarding, safety prequalification and procurement participation.
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated via Coupa. No public RFx captured.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 VIC GBE sweep; Melbourne Water runs panels and RFTs (e.g. Field Civil Panel, engineering services, water pipe/valve supply).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated VIC water GBE; confirm live tender listing directly (snippet evidence via aggregator listings used only as a discovery signal).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 mining sweep; re-checked this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated; individual sourcing events require an authenticated Ariba/SBN account.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 construction channel; re-checked this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Free trade account required to view live package closing dates; head-contractor to subcontractor work packages.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical local-government channel; re-checked this run (Inner West Summer Hill EOI + Thirning Villa captured).
discovered_sources.notes: Near-universal council adoption; registration required to download documents/submit.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE search for electricity transmission networks; Transgrid runs all sourcing (RFI/RFQ/RFP/RFT) through SAP Ariba branded "ibuy".
discovered_sources.notes: Suppliers must register on SAP Ariba Network to participate; individual events are registration-gated, so the portal is recorded rather than individual opportunities.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE search for distribution networks; Ausgrid uses portal.tenderlink.com/Ausgrid as its own e-tendering tool (buyer-hosted, not the aggregator product).
discovered_sources.notes: A BESS (Battery Energy Storage System) Delivery Panel opportunity was referenced; package detail is registration-gated. Source = the buyer’s own Ausgrid TenderLink portal, not a paid aggregator.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 public-research-body search; CSIRO publishes procurements over $400k via AusTender and runs a supplier portal.
discovered_sources.notes: Tenders above $400k go through AusTender; the SAP Ariba-hosted CSIRO Supplier Portal was flagged for discontinuance. Check AusTender for live CSIRO ATMs.

discovered_sources.source_name: TAFE NSW — Supplying to TAFE NSW
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 TAFE/education search; TAFE NSW lists open opportunities on the buy.nsw opportunities hub and invites sourcing events via SAP Business Network.
discovered_sources.notes: Open opportunities surface on buy.nsw; some sourcing events are issued via SAP Business Network (registration-gated).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 transport/logistics search; Qantas runs supplier onboarding and sourcing through the Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP), free to suppliers.
discovered_sources.notes: Opportunities are issued to suppliers via Coupa (registration-gated/invitation); no publicly-advertised RFx visible without login. Portal recorded, no tenders fabricated.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 retail search; Coles transacts with goods-not-for-resale suppliers via the Ariba Network (free standard account); product sourcing via RangeMe.
discovered_sources.notes: GNFR tenders run through Ariba Network; product suppliers via RangeMe. Registration-gated; recorded as a portal, no tenders fabricated.

discovered_sources.source_name: Woolworths Group — Suppliers (SAP Ariba / Partner Hub)

discovered_sources.notes: Non-trade sourcing via SAP Ariba; Partner Hub for trade suppliers. Registration-gated; recorded as a portal, no tenders fabricated.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Previously discovered; re-checked this run — two live tenders captured (Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre EOI; Thirning Villa refurbishment).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated council channel on VendorPanel; contact via the VendorPanel online forum (Frank Tambosis, Senior Procurement Business Partner).

discovered_sources.source_name: Reserve Bank of Australia — Procurement & Suppliers
